traits.name: Software & Coding
traits.description: Programming, software engineering, and open source development.

traits.name: Hardware & Electronics
traits.description: Robotics, microcontrollers, embedded systems, and circuit design.

traits.name: Web & App Development
traits.description: Frontend, backend, mobile apps, and user interface design.

traits.name: AI & Data Science
traits.description: Machine learning, artificial intelligence, and data analytics.

traits.name: Competitive Programming
traits.description: Algorithms, data structures, hackathons, and competitive coding.

traits.name: Robotics & Automotive
traits.description: Autonomous vehicles, mechanical robotics, and motorsport.

traits.name: Aerospace & Drones
traits.description: Aeronautics, rocketry, drones, and space technology.

traits.name: Public Speaking & Debating
traits.description: Public speaking, debating, Model UN, and literary activities.

traits.name: Model UN & Journalism
traits.description: Model United Nations, campus journalism, and editorial writing.

traits.name: Entrepreneurship & Startups
traits.description: Business ideation, pitching, incubation, and startup leadership.

traits.name: Music & Jamming
traits.description: Vocal, instrumental music, band performances, and jamming.

traits.name: Dance & Theatre
traits.description: Dance, drama, stage plays, and performing arts.

traits.name: Design & Photography
traits.description: Graphic design, photography, filmmaking, and visual media.

traits.name: Gaming & Esports
traits.description: Competitive gaming, game development, and esports tournaments.

traits.name: Social & Community
traits.description: Community outreach, event organizing, and campus life.

traits.name: Creative Expression
traits.description: Creative writing, art, and artistic projects.

traits.name: Low Commitment
traits.description: Requires light involvement (e.g. less than 2 hours weekly).

questions.text: What pulls you in the most?

options.text: Building tech — software, AI, or hardware.
Performing — music, dance, theatre, or sports.
Creating — design, writing, film, or photography.
Leading — business, events, community, or social impact.

questions.text: How comfortable are you writing code?

options.text: Very — it's basically my hobby.
Somewhat — I use it when needed.
Not really — I prefer other things.

questions.text: Does performing or speaking in front of people energize you?

options.text: Yes — I love the spotlight.
Sometimes — depends on the context.
Not really — I prefer working behind the scenes.

questions.text: Do you enjoy building or fixing physical things?

options.text: Yes — robots, circuits, engines, that kind of thing.
A bit — more into digital products than physical ones.
Not my thing — I'd rather create or connect.

questions.text: How do you prefer to work?

options.text: In a big, active team — energy feeds me.
Small focused group — quality over quantity.
Mostly solo, with occasional collaboration.

questions.text: What kind of impact do you want from a club?

options.text: Win competitions or represent the university.
Build real skills and a strong portfolio.
Meet interesting people and have a good time.
Give back — volunteer or community work.

questions.text: How much time can you realistically give per week?

options.text: 5+ hours — I want to go all in.
2-5 hours — solid but balanced.
Under 2 hours — just dipping my toes.
Staring at code all day.
Performing or public speaking.
Heavy physical or engineering work.
All-day meetings and admin.
